figure_descriptions.description: This figure illustrates a flow chemistry system comprising syringes feeding reactants into a packed bed reactor (PBR), followed by a sampling valve and analysis via HPLC. A backpressure regulator (BPR) is used to maintain system pressure.

engineering_logic.batch_limitation: Homogeneous Pd catalysis often requires catalyst loadings in the order of mol % to achieve effective coupling and suffers from catalyst re-use and recycling problems.
engineering_logic.flow_advantage: Emulsions offer a high interfacial area for reaction and ultrastability during reaction, with easy recovery of catalyst particles and products.

notes: The text discusses the use of Pickering emulsions for heterogeneous catalysis and mentions an outlook on the continuous flow synthesis of pharmaceuticals valsartan and sacubitril but doesn't provide detailed experimental conditions or exact process design parameters.

engineering_logic.batch_limitation: The neutralization of the reaction mixture with TEA leads to the formation of large amounts of a side product.
engineering_logic.flow_advantage: Does not require any off-line purification of intermediates and uses only phase extraction which can be readily automated.

figure_descriptions.description: This is a flow diagram illustrating a chemical reaction setup involving two feeds combined in a coil reactor. The setup includes catalysts and high-temperature conditions, followed by a back-pressure regulator.
figure_descriptions.key_information: Feed A: toluene:ethanol (4:3), flow rate 147 μL/min; Feed B: 1.6 M aq. K3PO4, flow rate 53 μL/min; 4 mL SS Coil at 150°C for 20 min; BPR at 17 bar.

figure_descriptions.description: This figure shows a flow chemistry setup used for a reaction involving multiple feeds and a coil reactor. It illustrates the flow paths of different solutions being mixed and reacted under controlled conditions.
figure_descriptions.key_information: The reaction occurs in a 4 mL stainless steel coil at 50 °C with a residence time of 8 minutes. There are three feeds with specified flow rates, and a BPR is set at 7 bar.

figure_descriptions.description: The figure shows a flow reactor setup for a chemical reaction involving the H-Cube Pro system with a 10% Pd/C catalyst at 40°C. The starting material is pumped through the system at a flow rate of 1000 µL/min, resulting in a product collected at the end.
figure_descriptions.key_information: Flow rate is 1000 µL/min; Catalyst is 10% Pd/C; Temperature is 40°C.

figure_descriptions.description: The figure depicts a flow chemistry setup for a multi-step reaction process. It includes reactant feeds, catalytic reaction steps, separation, and purification processes.
figure_descriptions.key_information: Conditions include 150°C for 20 min and 50°C for 8 min. Components include a palladium catalyst and various reagents.

notes: The detailed process involves multistep reactions with a flow liquid-liquid extractor (FLLEX) for improved conversions without side products.

engineering_logic.flow_advantage: Automated feedback loops allow for rapid optimization and iterative experimentation.

figure_descriptions.description: The figure illustrates an automated flow chemistry setup for reagent selection, reaction, and analysis. It includes a process for droplet generation, flow reaction, and response surface model refinement.
figure_descriptions.key_information: Reagents include 1:1 acetone/water and 1.66 M DBU in THF. Continuous argon delivery is used, and quenching with online analysis is performed.

notes: The experimental text details a method for optimizing Suzuki–Miyaura cross-coupling reactions in a continuous flow system using a Teflon tube reactor and automated droplet handling.
